item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting take on cliches with some horrific undertones and I enjoyed the fact that Talbot was actually intelligent enough to figure out that it wasn't just a "simple psychosis".

Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really like the use of color and the way you display the info.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's an instance of "Talbots:" becoming "Talbot:"

+1 tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good skip - I always enjoy an original format screw.

One thing driving me nuts, though. It's spelled "Isaac" - there exists, to the best of my knowledge, no terrestrial language in which a name is spelled "Issac". Other than that, great job!

item.title: Re: No lie, I almost made one like this
item.content:encoded:
Imo if this was supposed to be a meta skip for the deletions in universe then I think it was poorly executed. Maybe it's because I read it first but this article just feels like 3309 but significantly more confusing and convoluted, I didn't understand who Moqu Sen was and what his purpose was, and I certainly didn't understand why Talbot is still talking to us when he had already been erased.

Just my two cents

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "NoOneIsReadingThisAnyways"

item.title: Re: No lie, I almost made one like this
item.content:encoded:
Two things;

First! I'm glad you like the skip! I am working on revamping it, however. So, don't worry too terribly about the log errors. Tbh, I don't think I'll even be keeping the Lambert log.

Second!

Show Concept Spoilers!
– hide block

This… Actually isn't an "Error 404" skip. The errors occur as most of the data has been deleted. The skip itself is simply characters in-universe remembering objects, persons, and events which were summarily deleted.

The story of the skip is what happens to correct those in-universe constructs, with Moqu Sen being a stand-in for the actual deletions process occurring in-universe. Talbot is a character from two previously deleted tales who stuck around (just a bit of lore there for you ;P) and has found himself tagged for deletion. Sen fails to delete him, because the article that brought Sen into existence in-universe is the same one that keeps Talbot around. Sen can't delete Talbot without deleting an article lodged firmly in the +100's.

So, if you wanna write an error 404 skip? Go nuts bro :P

item.title: No lie, I almost made one like this
item.content:encoded:
I have a ton of monotonous tasks at work and about a week ago I thought "what if I made a 404 Error SCP?"
Glad to see someone far more skilled than I am beat me to it to give the concept justice (I am NOT claiming this was stolen, copied, etc… just to clarify, haha).

One thing I want to suggest, though, it that you may want to remove the points in the log that go,
"Sen: "Lambert. I need you to take a deep breath. Breathe."

Lambert: "…"

Sen: "Just breathe, Lambert."

I don't think the "…" are really necessary, as they aren't responding.

Still, great work.
I really enjoyed this SCP and will definitely upvote it.

item.title: Re: He's lying to you all.
item.content:encoded: This is significantly more interesting than school. thanks :)

item.title: Your favorite antagonist(?) now has fanart.
item.content:encoded:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-10434815/hopefully-regularily-updated-scp-fan-art#post-4169596

The lovely Crashington has made some smashing fanart of Moqu and I figured I'd share it with all of you. :) Go give it a look-see!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
good stuff, simple and very effective interview logs …

overall a great first article ! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for Chinese punchline.

item.title: Kry Decides To Celebrate a Thing! 🎉
item.content:encoded:
🎉WE HAVE ACHEIVED

100 UPBOATS at the time of posting !!! 🎉

Y'all are absolutely wonderful people. I did not think my first skip would go to +100 in under two months. Is celebrating a little silly? Kinda, but I'm gonna do it anyways. Everyone likes party horn confetti emojis anyhow.

Thank you all- and don't think I've forgotten about those edits I promised! I swear they'll happen soon, rewriting the Talbot/Sen log to better reflect the intended ending is kinda kicking my ass. Talbot's extremely snarky and brash demeanor is hard to write because it's easy to overdo it, and Lambert's log will be cut entirely so… Ye.

You all rock though. :) 'nuff said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude. Read 1848 and report back to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm new to the SCP community, and I would actually consider this to be my favorite SCP currently. SO GOOD.

item.title: Re: He's lying to you all.
item.content:encoded: Just read this. Freakin incredible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I think there's a bit of early roughness, but Talbot's log kind of smoothed it out and, much like him, brought me to acceptance. +1

I especially like the "am I the first actual scientist you brought in here?" Thing. Always good to see actual scientific thinking.

One note: "anti-memetic" is an adjective. The objects themselves (noun form) are anti-memes or antimemes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pleasantly surprised while reading to find a crosslink to SCP-4249. Thanks, Kry!

Thoroughly enjoyed the skip. Not what I was expecting going into it, that's for sure.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possibly what he does is turn you into the equivalent of 055 … you do still exist and can interact with the scenery (including the computers), but no-one else other than the last person you saw before his intercession will ever acknowledge your existence ever again. And he makes sure to get to work on them as soon as they're identified, whether through some anomalous compulsion, or maybe just heavy amnestics.

That or we're actually witnessing the actions of Isaacs' ghost.

item.title: Rewrite announcement (edited)
item.content:encoded:
Edited (AGAIN):

Buckle up bois cause ol' Salems here is gonna revamp this shit out of this after ClicheCon.

CSS fuckary, log rewrites, added content, fixing the obvious plotholes…

Now, this isn't a major rewrite. The biggest change is going to be the slashing of unneeded content (Looking at Lamberts log here) in exchange for a better and more concise ending. …also aesthetic fixes. This is my focus because the revelation I intended readers to have is so poorly executed that NO ONE has gotten it (at least no one who commented.)

That is not the fault of any of you. The blame is on me, because it's my poor execution hat has led most of you to go "Salem, what the fuck is this."

…So, expect the fixes sometime in late February. Have a good day lovelies~ <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact that Talbots was genre savvy enough to come to the obvious conclusion in his log. That being said, Lambert's log wasn't particularly interesting and the format doesn't really do much to assuage this. The article doesn't really give enough to work with either. If Talbots was erased, then why is he able to dig this up? I also don't really care much about Môqù since I barely know anything. What's their end goal? Do they work for the Foundation or are they an outside agent? Why can't they spend five minutes to research psychosis so they don't get outed so easily? I was kinda surprised it ended where it did because I was expecting it to continue and give me some more context with the entire thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, thank you! I tried other formats of login to restrict the log in code, but none of them worked.

I'm glad you enjoyed the skip though! ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Logs in no matter what you put in. the literally unreadable was just some humor. Loved the scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …come again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still logs you in no matter what you put in the login/password. literally unreadable…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is my issue as well. The entire middle, up until the last line of dialogue could easily be cut. The decision to begin with a log-in screen was an example of pointless format screw that really rubs me the wrong way on pretty much any article, as was the mystery narrator.

I ultimately downvoted, but this feels like a good idea in need of a bit of streamlining. If/when that happens, I will happily reverse my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made a dialogue change that- I hope- makes it clearer that the voice of the green text is Issac Talbot. That should answer the question of why he's helping. With the clearer link, it should also show how he went from being bullheaded in the log to the more reserved and timid tone he uses in the green text

As for the lack of tension and the belaboured point… Not only am I unsure of how to fix that, but I believe it'll have to wait until cliche-con ends. I'm not allowed to make major edits at this point, so I can't quite do much. My apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is decent (other than "decern", which should be "discern", or more properly "distinguish"), but the point is awfully belaboured, and is pretty obvious from quite early on. Not to mention that there are two distinct actors here, and the one that isn't Sen doesn't receive any characterisation - why is it helping (?) the Foundation? Ultimately this doesn't build up any real tension from the premise, unfortunately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff. A nice, subtle and underlying terror in this one.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm glad you enjoy it- and thanks again for looking it over in chat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved this when I saw it in chat, and I love it now. Easy +1

item.title: He's lying to you all.
item.content:encoded:
Hello Lovlies! KrySalems here with my firstever skip… which also happens to be my Cliche-con entry!

I would just like to thank some lovely folks who made this skip possible- LadyKatie was quite helpful with critique while surbet11, demifiend and Tracque_ gave it a quick read over with their yay/nay’s.

As this is for Cliche-con, I’d like to specify that the cliches are; POI gets one over on the Foundation (manages to cover up an SCP), super scary murder monster, and compulsion. Compulsion is minor and the murder monster is… not what you may expect.

This is a Catagory 3 entry!

Enjoy!
